brief_justification: The XML contains only metadata (title, abstract, keywords, and references) without the full article body. Based on the available abstract and references, this is a descriptive study of protest events during the COVID-19 pandemic in Brazil (2020-2021) using Protest Event Analysis methodology with newspaper-sourced data covering all five regions. It maps which social groups protested and their demands, finding prominence of workers' protests, right-wing mobilization, and local victims' activism against violence and racism. It is not an empirical quantitative paper — it uses qualitative/descriptive event cataloging rather than statistical modeling.
